ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has an entry for fried chicken food product (FOODON_00002490); the combined set meal with fries is not individually coded. Closest parent match used for the primary protein component; the composite set meal (fried chicken + fries) has no exact FoodOn URI.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers fried chicken meat. The composite set meal (fried chicken + fries) does not have a single FoodEx2 code; the dominant protein component code is used as the closest parent anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite fast-food set meal.

serving.description: 1 set meal (3 pieces fried chicken + 1 medium packet fries)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a set meal comprising 3 pieces of deep-fried battered chicken (approximately 300 g) and one medium packet of french fries (approximately 150 g), totalling approximately 450 g.

nutrient_source.title: Fried chicken (3 pc) with fries — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo) for the composite record 'Fried chicken (3 pc) with fries' (cr_id F08020028). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB entry at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy reference for fried chicken with fries composite as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — French fries and fried chicken composite estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this exact composite set meal. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate contributor: french fries have a published GI of approximately 63–75 (University of Sydney GI Database; mean ~65 for deep-fried potato strips). The battered fried chicken contributes refined carbohydrate from the batter (GI ~70 for wheat-flour batter analogues) but at lower carbohydrate mass. A weighted composite GI of 65 is applied. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 68.1) / 100 ≈ 44.3 g GL (based on 450 g serving × 15.13 g carbohydrate/100 g = 68.1 g available carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (526 mg/100 g) and saturated fat from double deep-frying
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal; sodium at 526 mg/100 g delivers approximately 2,367 mg sodium per 450 g serving — nearly the entire recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg — and the saturated fat from deep-frying further promotes arterial stiffness and elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL ~44 per serving), low dietary fibre (1.27 g/100 g), and high fat content worsening insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this meal; the high glycemic load of approximately 44 per serving drives rapid postprandial glucose elevation, while the low fibre content (1.27 g/100 g) provides minimal glucose-buffering, and the high fat content from deep-frying contributes to insulin resistance — choose grilled chicken with a high-fibre side instead.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.5 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.3 g/100 g), and very high sodium (526 mg/100 g) from double deep-frying
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal; the combination of saturated fat, trans fat from deep-frying, and very high sodium (approximately 2,367 mg per serving) significantly elevates LDL cholesterol, promotes endothelial dysfunction, and raises blood pressure, all of which increase cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory AGEs and trans fat from double deep-frying, high sodium, and nightshade potato
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this meal; deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and trans fat that are strongly pro-inflammatory, sodium at 526 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg RA threshold and may worsen tissue swelling, and potato fries are a nightshade food that some RA patients report worsens joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fried Chicken (3 pieces) with French Fries is a Western-style fast-food set meal widely sold in Singapore at quick-service restaurant chains and hawker-adjacent stalls. The meal comprises three pieces of bone-in chicken (typically a combination of drumstick, thigh, and wing) coated in a seasoned wheat-flour batter and deep-fried in vegetable or palm oil until golden and crispy, served alongside one medium packet of deep-fried potato french fries. The chicken exterior is crunchy and well-seasoned with salt and spice blends, while the interior meat is juicy and tender. The fries are crisp, lightly salted, and starchy. Per 100 g, the meal provides 247 kcal, 15.44 g protein, 13.81 g fat, and 15.13 g carbohydrate, with 526 mg sodium — a high sodium load. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium) driven primarily by the deep-fried potato fries, and the glycemic load per 450 g serving is approximately 44, which is very high. Dietary fibre is low at 1.27 g/100 g. Clinically, this meal is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension (very high sodium per serving), type 2 diabetes (high glycemic load, low fibre, high fat), cardiovascular disease (saturated and trans fat, high sodium), or rheumatoid arthritis (pro-inflammatory AGEs, trans fat, nightshade potato). It is halal-certified in most Singapore fast-food contexts. The meal is commonly consumed as a working lunch or casual dinner and is culturally associated with American-style fast food adapted for the Singapore market.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo) canonical record F08020028 at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy fried chicken and fries composite reference. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food (deep-fried potato strips, GI ~65). All primary nutrient data from an approved primary regional source.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (3.5 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy reference for fried chicken with fries composite, as the HPB record does not separately report saturated fat. This is an approved secondary source adaptation. 2. Trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) is carried forward from the input health_suitability caution note and is consistent with USDA data for deep-fried battered chicken products; this value should be confirmed against a direct HPB or USDA measurement when available. 3. The following per_100g fields are set to -1 because detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for this composite set meal are not separately reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this exact preparation as of 2026: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Source consulted and confirmed unavailable: HPB Singapore Foodo database (https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/) and USDA FoodData Central (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/) as of April 2026; limited published data for this composite fast-food preparation.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: GI (65), gi_category (medium), glycemic_load_per_serving (44.3), and gi_source are all present. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for deep-fried potato strips with documented estimation basis.

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' to satisfy g5_ontology_honesty.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record F08020028 (Fried Chicken 3 pieces with French Fries) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard. G3 numeric hygiene: all 21 fields set to -1 are fully documented in notes with reason and source citation. G5 ontology honesty: match_type values corrected to 'closest_parent' (a valid enumerated value). All mandatory Singapore multilingual names present. Core macros sourced from HPB Foodo. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database. Health context covers all three required conditions. LLM description exceeds 200 characters. Aliases are comprehensive and internally consistent between aliases_by_language and flat aliases array.
